LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AUTO RESCUE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION. (THE COURT NOTES THAT THE NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER REFER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "NEGLIGENCE." THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THIS WAS A MISTAKE, AND THAT MOVING PARTY WAS REFERRING TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION. THE OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER IS DIRECTED TO THE FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION.) PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT LEARN OF THE FACTS SHOWING THAT MR. AGUILAR COULD BE LIABLE UNTIL PLAINTIFF'S INVESTIGATOR INTERVIEWED THE EYEWITNESS IN DECEMBER 2012. PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES IGNORANCE WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 474 AND THE DOE AMENDMENT WAS PROPER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE AN INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION OR ACTIVE CONCEALMENT CAUSE OF ACTION THAT IS UNRELATED TO DEFENDANT'S PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATIONS WITH THE POLICE. SEE HAGBERG V. CAL. FED BANK FSB (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 350; HUNSUCKER V. SUNNYVALE HILTON INN (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 1498. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CAUSE OF ACTION RELATES BACK TO THE ORIGINAL FILING OF THE COMPLAINT. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE DEMURRER TO THE FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS REGARDING HIS DISCOVERY OF THE FRAUD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

BALDINI MAY FILE AND SERVE AN SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION AND INMAN AUTO RESCUE MAY FILE AND SERVE ITS REPLY IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 1005(B).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES L. ARMSTRONG OF THE BENNETT JOHNSON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 444-5020 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SANDRA CALIN OF KRAMER DEBOER & KEANE, TEL. NO. 933-7855 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AUTO RESUCE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT- The Court denied the motion to strike in light of the ruling on the demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES L. ARMSTRONG OF THE BENNETT JOHNSON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 444-5020 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SANDRA CALIN OF KRAMER DEBOER & KEANE, TEL. NO. 933-7855 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AUTO RESCUE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The Court overrules the demurrer to the second cause of action for fraudulent misrepresentation. (The Court notes that the Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer refer to the second cause of action for "negligence." The Court assumes that this was a mistake, and that moving party was referring to the second cause of action for fraudulent misrepresentation. The opposition to the demurrer is directed to the fraudulent misrepresentation cause of action.) Plaintiff alleges a cause of action for fraud as to this defendant. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege an intentional misrepresentation or active concealment cause of action that is unrelated to defendant's privileged communications with the police. See Hagberg v. Cal. Fed Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350; Hunsucker v. Sunnyvale Hilton Inn (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1498. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-11-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES L. ARMSTRONG OF BENNETT JOHNSON, TEL. NO. (510) 444-5020 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SANDRA CALIN OF KRAMER DEBOER & KEANE, TEL. NO. 933-7855 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT INMAN'S AUTO RESCUE, L.P.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to December 11, 2013 in order to permit Mr. Baldini to conduct discovery on the limited jurisdictional issues presented by the motion. (HealthMarkets, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Berman) (2009) 171 CA4th 1160, 1173.) Mr. Baldini may file and serve an supplemental opposition and Inman Auto Rescue may file and serve its reply in accordance with CCP 1005(b).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Continued To Aug-28-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: james armstrong, esq., 510.444.5020, for plaintiff dino baldini.
jody struck, esq., 510.763.2324, for defendant luis aguilar.
sanaz shamsian, esq., 818.657.0255, for defendant auto rescue of san francisco, llc.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the negligence cause of action. The cause of action relates back to the original filing of the complaint. The Court sustains with 15 days leave to amend the demurrer to the fraudulent misrepresentation cause of action to plead facts regarding his discovery of the fraud. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: james armstrong, esq., 510.444.5020, for plaintiff dino baldini.
jody struck, esq., 510.763.2324, for defendant luis aguilar.
sanaz shamsian, esq., 818.657.0255, for defendant auto rescue of san francisco, llc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT LUIS AGUILAR MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. Plaintiff did not learn of the facts showing that Mr. Aguilar could be liable until plaintiff's investigator interviewed the eyewitness in December 2012. Plaintiff demonstrates ignorance within the meaning of CCP 474 and the Doe amendment was proper. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
